title: Altamiranoa parviflora
intro: Altamiranoa parviflora is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Altamiranoa parviflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Altamiranoa parviflora is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the broader category of flowering plants. Further categorization would involve identifying its order, family, and genus more specifically. Specifically, the classification of Altamiranoa parviflora is within the order Asterales and family Asteraceae. This classification can provide a more detailed understanding of the plant's evolutionary history and relationships to other plants.
